reasoning: The EFFR on Nov 7 (which applies to Sunday Nov 9) is firmly at 3.87% following the Oct 29 FOMC rate cut. During mid-month periods with no FOMC meetings, the EFFR typically remains flat, particularly given the recent end of Quantitative Tightening (QT), which eliminates upward pressure on overnight funding. Therefore, an intra-month increase to 3.88% or higher by Nov 16 or Dec 9 is highly unlikely. For Feb 7, 2026, the Fed is expected to either pause at this level or cut rates further; a sudden rate hike by January 2026 contradicting their recent move is very improbable, keeping the chance of an increase low, though uncertainty naturally rises for the furthest date.
batch_queries: Will the effective federal funds rate (interest rate) set by the Federal Reserve have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-11-09?
